title: Grapes

overview.description: Small, juicy fruits that grow in bunches and come in many colours, flavours and varieties.

method: Remove the grapes from the stem if desired.

learning.did-you-know: Grapes grow in bunches on vines, and there are thousands of different grape varieties. Grapes can be eaten fresh, dried into raisins or pressed to make juice.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How grapes grow and how the same fruit can be eaten fresh, dried or turned into other foods and drinks.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A convenient, naturally sweet fruit snack that introduces children to varieties, growing vines and the way drying can transform fresh fruit into a completely different food.
chapman-tips: Grapes can be green, red, purple, black or other colours depending on the variety.
Some grapes have seeds and some are seedless.
Grapes can taste sweet, sour or a mixture of both.
Raisins are grapes that have been dried, losing much of their water.
Grapes can be frozen and make a cold, sweet snack.
notes.leftovers: Grapes can be frozen, dried into raisins or used in fruit salads, desserts and savoury salads.
